pvp-updates-competitors-cup-2026.text: Thank you for your continued passion for Trainer Battles in Pokémon GO. Earlier this year, we shared details about exciting updates coming to the Pokémon GO PvP battle system in our PvP update announcement. With this update, the team sought to strengthen Trainer Battles by rebuilding the core battle system so that battles consistently reflect player skill while maintaining the fast, responsive feel players expect from Pokémon GO.

Following the Preview Cup, extensive analysis of battle data, and consideration of Trainer feedback regarding gameplay speed, we’re excited to announce that the newPvP battle system is rolling out beginning today, June 23, 2026.
With this update, all previously announced cups in GO Battle League: Forever Forward will now take place using the new PvP battle system.

The updated system also opens the door for more Pokémon to join Trainer Battles. Beginning today, Mimikyu will now be eligible to battle in the GO Battle League!* We look forward to introducing even more new battle mechanics with the updated system.
As we continue evolving the Trainer Battle experience, we also recognize the dedication of Trainers preparing for the 2026 Pokémon World Championships.

Competitors around the world have spent the season refining strategies, practicing matchups, and building teams based on the current battle system. To support a consistent competitive environment through the conclusion of the Season, we are introducing Competitors Cup beginning today.
Competitors Cup will use the current PvP battle system and will remain available through Sunday, August 30, 2026, following the conclusion of the 2026 Pokémon World Championships.*
Since Competitors Cup uses the current system, Mimikyu will not be eligible for Competitors Cup.
Here are the key dates to keep in mind as we transition to the new Pokémon GO PvP battle system.

items: The new PvP battle system is now available for all Trainers.
All previously announced GO Battle League: Forever Forward cups will transition to the new system.
Competitors Cup, featuring the current PvP battle system, is now available. The 2026 Pokémon World Championships will be played using Competitors Cup.
Mimikyu is now eligible for GO Battle League battles outside of Competitors Cup.
Competitors Cup will conclude following the end of the 2026 Pokémon World Championships.

pvp-updates-competitors-cup-2026.text: We look forward to hearing your feedback as we continue to improve and strengthen the foundation of the GO Battle League!

road-of-legends-2026.text: July 6, 2026, at 12:01 a.m. to July 10, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. local time

The countdown to Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global starts here. During the Road of Legends, Trainers around the world can gear up for the big weekend with a full week of powerful raid encounters, event bonuses, and GO Pass rewards. Whether you’re looking to strengthen your team, stock up on rewards, or take on some of Pokémon GO’s most formidable foes, this is your chance to get ready for the adventure ahead.

Legendary Pokémon, Ultra Beasts, and other powerful Pokémon will appear in raids throughout the event. Featured Pokémon will also appear in Raid Hours and Mega Raid Hours each day, giving Trainers even more chances to battle, catch, and prepare for Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global.
Pokémon appearing in five-star raids each day will also be featured in a Raid Hour from 6:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. local time on that day. On Monday, Raid Hour will take place from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m.

Pokémon appearing in Mega Raids each day will also be featured in a Raid Hour from 7:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. local time on that day. On Friday, there will be a Primal Raid Hour from 7:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m.
The following Pokémon will appear in raids:
Pheromosa (Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and India)*
*If you’re lucky, you may encounter a Shiny one!
Note: Nihilego, Guzzlord, and Necrozma do not have regional locks and appear globally when active in 5-star raids.
Some Pokémon caught from these raids may know Charged Attacks that have an Adventure Effect.

items: Kyurem may know the Charged Attack Glaciate.
Origin Forme Palkia may know the Charged Attack Spacial Rend
Origin Forme Dialga may know the Charged Attack Roar of Time.

road-of-legends-2026.text: These Adventure Effects are available through Elite TM for the first time in Pokémon GO!

Get ready, Trainers! Available for a limited time in celebration of Pokémon GO’s 10th Anniversary, Trainers can use an Elite TM to get Spacial Rend or Roar of Time for Origin Forme Palkia and Origin Forme Dialga respectively during Road of Legends through Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global.

After catching certain Pokémon from Raid Battles, Trainers will have a chance to receive Special Backgrounds! These souvenir backgrounds will appear on a Pokémon’s summary page, similar to the Location Backgrounds that are available exclusively at in-person events.
Learn more about Special Backgrounds here!
Pokémon caught from five-star raids, Primal Raids, and Mega Raid Battles between July 6 and July 12, may also have a Special Background.

Mewtwo caught from Super Mega Raids during GO Fest: Global may have a Special Background. If that Mewtwo with a Special Background is Mega Evolved, its Mega Evolution’s background will have an additional visual effect.
Learn more about Mewtwo’s Mega Evolutions here.
Super Mega Raids During Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global
Did you forget? We didn’t think so! Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y will be featured in Super Mega Raids during Pokémon GO Fest: Global!

As a reminder, there will be a special bonus debuting during the global Pokémon GO Fest event: Every Mewtwo caught from Super Mega Raids will have at least one Mega Level already unlocked, and it can be Mega Evolved without the initial energy cost!* Catch up on the news here.
And there’s more! Mewtwo caught from Super Mega Raids will also be more likely to have high Attack, Defense, and HP.

Super Mega Raids are a type of Mega Raid in Pokémon GO where you band together with fellow Trainers to take on exceptionally powerful Mega-Evolved Pokémon. These difficult battles are best taken on with several other Trainers who all have at least one Mega-Evolved Pokémon in their battle party.
While these battles may be tough, successfully defeating them with your fellow Trainers will yield substantially more rewards than standard Mega Raids, including more Mega Energy!
Learn more about and prepare for Super Mega Raids here.

Super Mega Raids require major teamwork, and may only appear at certain Gyms! Use http://pokemongo.com/map or Campfire to find upcoming and active Super Mega Raids near you, and filter for community meetups to plan more seamlessly.
Hatch your way into GO Fest! Featured Eggs will be available starting Monday, July 6.
If you’re lucky, Larvesta may hatch from 2 km, 5 km, or 10 km Eggs!
*If you’re lucky, you may hatch a Shiny one!
These bonuses are active from the beginning of Road of Legends through Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global.

items: No Remote Raid Pass limit Monday, July 6, to Sunday, July 12, 2026.
Trainers level 31 and up are guaranteed to receive Candy XL for trading Pokémon.
Receive special stickers from spinning PokéStops and opening Gifts.
Premier Balls will be more effective at catching Pokémon after raids.
Party Power charges faster in raids.

GO Pass and GO Pass Deluxe
A GO Pass is a free, limited-time progression track available during a specified period. By collecting GO Points, you can increase your rank to earn rewards.

Trainers will automatically receive GO Pass: Flying Taxi on Tuesday, June 23, at 10:00 a.m. local time. You can collect GO Points and rank up to get additional rewards through Monday, June 29, at 8:00 p.m. local time.
Additionally, from Saturday, June 27, at 12:00 a.m. to Monday, June 29, at 7:59 p.m. local time, there’s no daily limit for how many GO Points you can earn!
For US$4.99*, Trainers can upgrade to the GO Pass Deluxe, a paid version of the GO Pass that offers upgraded rewards and faster progression.
For US$6.99*, Trainers can upgrade to the GO Pass Deluxe + 6 Ranks to also automatically earn enough GO Points to reach Rank 7.

While progressing through the GO Pass Deluxe, you’ll be able to claim all of the rewards from both the free GO Pass and GO Pass Deluxe. You can upgrade to a GO Pass Deluxe at any time and still collect rewards from previously unlocked ranks.
Rewards unlocked in the GO Pass will expire on Wednesday, July 1, at 7:59 p.m. local time, so be sure to claim your rewards before they’re gone!
*All prices listed are in USD or the equivalent pricing tier in your local currency.

Where can I find Super Mega Raids?
Super Mega Raids will take great teamwork and may only appear at certain Gyms, so we’re making it easier to find nearby Gyms, Power Spots, and more using a new web-based map.

You can visit pokemongo.com/map to see the locations where Super Mega Raids might appear during an event, in addition to those in progress. You can also filter the map to show nearby community meetups days in advance, allowing you to plan more seamlessly.

Plus, you’ll be able to view nearby community meetups through the map. If you’ve never attended a meetup before, Super Mega Raids are a great reason to go out and meet your local community of Trainers for the first time!
Catch up with the latest updates to Mega Evolution and Super Mega Raids here.
Looking for other Trainers to play with?
Struggling in Raids or Max Battles without enough Trainers by your side?
Find nearby meetups hosted by Community Ambassadors and join other Trainers to help you take on any challenge.
Find your community

Community Ambassadors receive support from Niantic to run Pokémon GO events for their communities. If there are no Community Ambassadors near you, you can also search for and find the meetups closest to you.
Coordinate more easily with friends, family, and other Trainers in your community, and raise the odds of success in battle together!

*All prices listed are in USD or the equivalent pricing tier in your local currency.

Looking for other Trainers to play with?
If you’re looking for Trainers in your area to trade, battle, or join raids with on Community Day, check out our Community Day map! Here you can find Community Ambassador events and local parks.

Community Ambassadors receive support from Niantic to run Pokémon GO events for their communities. If there are no Community Ambassadors near you, you can also search Campfire and find other meetups in your area.

The parks marked on the Community Day map are historically popular places for Trainers to gather during Community Day. These parks could be a fun option to play with your local community while enjoying some fresh air.* We hope this map helps Trainers connect!

*Popular locations are provided for informational purposes only. Niantic is not hosting or operating official or unofficial events at these locations. Please be aware of your surroundings when playing Niantic games.
Check in to a Community Ambassador event to get Timed Research that leads to additional encounters with Frigibax with a Special Background, as well as other premium item rewards!
Starting in January 2026, the featured Pokémon for Community Days can have a yearly Special Background.
Community Day voting is back for August Community Day!

We’re bringing back Community Day voting for the final Community Day of the Season! From Saturday, May 30, at 6:00 p.m. PDT to Sunday, May 31, at 6:00 p.m. PDT, you’ll be able to vote for the August Community Day featured Pokémon via our official Pokémon GO YouTube channel. The Pokémon that receives the most votes will be featured on August Community Day on August 16, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. local time.
August Community Day Candidates
Your Community Day candidates are Nickit, Yamper, Fidough, and Tadbulb!
The winner will be announced after voting closes, and event bonuses and more event details will be announced later. Stay tuned, Trainers!
How to Vote
We will host a poll on our official Pokémon GO YouTube channel on Saturday, May 30, at 6:00 p.m. PDT to Sunday, May 31, at 6:00 p.m. PDT.
You will have 24 hours to cast your vote for the Pokémon you’d like to see most for August Community Day 2026. Each Trainer gets one vote!
The winners will be announced shortly after voting ends. The Pokémon that receives the most votes by the end of the voting period will be the featured Pokémon for August Community Day.
Make your voices heard for this upcoming Community Day! Be sure to campaign for your favorite Pokémon using #PokemonGOCommunityDay.
Let’s GO!

A GO Pass is a free, limited-time progression track available during a specified period. By collecting GO Points, you can increase your rank to earn rewards.

mega-mewtwo-gofest-2026.text: Trainers,
Exciting content debuts during Pokémon GO Fest 2026!
Pokémon Debuts
Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y, the Mega Evolutions of the Genetic Pokémon Mewtwo, will make their Pokémon GO debuts during Pokémon GO Fest 2026!
How to Mega Evolve Mewtwo
Exciting news, Trainers! Your Mewtwo can now Mega Evolve, including ones already caught.

Mewtwo can now Mega Evolve into Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y. Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y are exceptionally powerful Pokémon that require much more Mega Energy than other Pokémon to Mega Evolve. This energy will become available for the first time from Mega Mewtwo Super Mega Raids during Pokémon GO Fest 2026.

As a special bonus with its debut during Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global, every Mega Mewtwo caught from Super Mega Raids will also have at least one Mega Level already unlocked and can be Mega Evolved without the initial energy cost.*
*if you’re lucky, it might even be at Mega Level 2 or 3!
Mewtwo’s Mega Evolution Energy
Mega Mewtwo X Energy and Mega Mewtwo Y Energy are rare resources that can be used to Mega Evolve Mewtwo into either of its two powerful temporary forms.

Trainers who attend in-person GO Fest events this year can get early access to either Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y! The City Experience in Tokyo, Chicago, and Copenhagen includes branching Timed Research that awards an encounter with Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y that have the first Mega Level already unlocked!

Trainers who attend the Park Session can also take on Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y at the end of their park session. More communication about the location within the park will be shared closer to the event.

Starting during Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global, you’ll be able to collect these rare types of energy by defeating Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y in Super Mega Raid Battles. During the event, Trainers can also collect Mega Energy by completing GO Fest Timed Research. Mewtwo caught from Super Mega Battles will have at least one Mega Level already unlocked!*

items: Defeat Mega Mewtwo X in Super Mega Raids to get the energy you need to Mega Evolve Mewtwo into Mega Mewtwo X.
Defeat Mega Mewtwo Y in Super Mega Raids to get the energy you need to Mega Evolve Mewtwo into Mega Mewtwo Y.

mega-mewtwo-gofest-2026.text: *If you’re lucky, it might even be at Mega Level 2 or 3!
Additional Mega Evolution System Updates
Mega Mewtwo’s arrival isn’t the only big thing coming to Mega Evolution in Pokémon GO!
Faster Mega Level Progression
In an upcoming update, Trainers will have the option to use their extra Mega Energy to quickly level up their Pokémon’s Mega Levels without needing to wait for the daily progression cooldown.
Split Mega Level Progression

For Pokémon with multiple Mega Evolutions, such as Mewtwo, progression will now be tracked individually for each Mega Evolution. For example, the Mega Level for Mega Mewtwo X will increase independently from the Mega Level for Mega Mewtwo Y.

For Mega Charizard X and Mega Charizard Y, any Charizard that you have Mega Evolved will carry over its Mega Level to both Mega Evolutions after this split. Charizard’s Mega Evolutions will also require separate Mega Energy going forward, which you can get from taking on either Mega Charizard X or Mega Charizard Y in Mega Raids. If you already have Charizard Mega Energy, you will be awarded that amount of both Mega Charizard X Energy and Mega Charizard Y Energy after this split.
Learn more about other updates to Mega Evolution here.
Featured Attacks
All Mewtwo caught during in-person GO Fest events by ticket holders will know the Fast Attack Counter and the Charged Attack Psystrike.
During in-person GO Fest events, only ticket holders can catch Mewtwo with the featured attacks.
During GO Fest: Global

items: Mewtwo caught from Mega Mewtwo X Super Mega raids will know the Fast Attack Counter.
Mewtwo caught from Mega Mewtwo Y Super Mega raids will know the Charged Attack Psystrike.

mega-mewtwo-gofest-2026.text: Want to master Mega Evolution and prepare for Mega Mewtwo’s arrival?

Ticket-holding attendees of in-person Pokémon GO Fest events will have the opportunity to encounter a Mewtwo that has at least the first Mega Level unlocked!
Timed Research in City Experience
Become a Pokémon GO expert during in-person GO Fest events!

Trainers who have tickets to in-person GO Fest events—either the full GO Fest ticket or the City Explorer ticket—will receive four Timed Research opportunities on their first day of city gameplay. Each Timed Research is a Trainer Challenge based in one of four City Districts, focusing on a different aspect of becoming a great Pokémon GO Trainer. Each Trainer Challenge will award a unique medal commemorating the accomplishment. Trainers will need to explore at least two City Districts and complete the Trainer Challenge there to achieve the in-game Pokémon GO Expert medal. Only Trainers attending the in-person Pokémon GO Fest events will be able to complete the Timed Research and earn this special commemorative in-game medal.
The Pokémon GO Expert medal requires Trainers to physically explore the unique traits of each host city through Pokémon GO and complete the Trainer Challenge associated with each City District.
If a Trainer completes at least two Trainer Challenges, they can choose to encounter either Mega Mewtwo X or Mega Mewtwo Y with its first Mega Level unlocked.
Trainers playing in the same GO Fest city on multiple days will get only one set of Timed Research (including the bonus Timed Research) and rewards.
Trainers playing in different GO Fest cities will get one set of Timed Research (including the bonus Timed Research) and rewards per city—up to three sets total.
The City Experience Timed Research will be active for the duration of each event at the times listed below.
Pokémon GO Fest: Tokyo – Monday, May 25, at 12:00 a.m. through Monday, June 1, at 11:59 p.m. local time
Pokémon GO Fest: Chicago – Thursday, June 4, at 12:00 a.m. through Sunday, June 7, at 11:59 p.m. local time
Pokémon GO Fest: Copenhagen – Thursday, June 11, at 12:00 a.m. through Sunday, June 14, at 11:59 p.m. local time
Participate in a massive Mega Mewtwo raid at your Pokémon GO Fest park session.

Ticket-holding Pokémon GO Fest attendees are invited to join a Super Mega Raid at the end of their ticketed park session. For the last thirty minutes of the Park Experience, new Gyms will appear that allow over one thousand Trainers to take on Mega Mewtwo together!
Both Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y will appear during this part of the event. Stay alert, Trainers—your help is essential to keep Team GO Rocket away from Mewtwo!
Mewtwo caught from the Super Mega Raids will have a Pokémon GO Fest 2026 Location Background and the first Mega Level unlocked!*
More information will be shared to ensure as many Trainers as possible can participate.
Be sure to join this exciting experience and leave Pokémon GO Fest with new Pokémon and exciting memories.

pgo-pokemon-fossil-musuem-chicago-2026.text: Pokémon GO at the Pokémon Fossil Museum in Chicago

A paleontology-themed Pokémon exhibition is debuting in North America at Chicago's Field Museum! The Pokémon Fossil Museum runs from Friday, May 22, 2026, through Sunday, April 11, 2027, open 9:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. CDT.
The experience brings together the worlds of Pokémon and paleontology, letting visitors compare creatures like Tyrantrum and Archeops against authentic fossils — including SUE the T. rex specimen.
